ideation: This animation sequence simulates the action of pushing a pin into a surface. It starts with both arms extended forward, then one arm moves down to simulate the pushing action, and finally both arms return to a neutral position.  The sequence uses a combination of joint1 (roll) and joint2 (pitch) for natural-looking movements. The use of both arms helps to emphasize the "pushing" motion, making it more expressive than if only one arm was used. The final frame is a return to a neutral position.

frames.beat: arms extend forward, ready to push
left arm pushes down with force
both arms return to neutral, pin is set
